beats.narration: Everything radiography does happens at a scale you can't see: the atom. Before sources and exposures, meet the cast.

payload.title: INSIDE THE ATOM
payload.sub: The cast of characters behind every radiograph.

beats.narration: Elements are matter's building blocks. The atom is the smallest piece that's still that element. Atoms bond into molecules; different elements bonded make compounds.
In the nucleus: protons, one positive charge each, and neutrons, no charge. Both weigh about one mass unit. Electrons orbit in shells, negative and featherweight.
In a neutral atom, electrons equal protons. Strip one away and you get an ion. Ionization exposes film, fires survey meters, damages tissue.

payload.heading: Lose an electron, become an ion

beats.narration: Lock in three facts. Proton: positive, mass one. Neutron: no charge, mass one. Electron: negative, almost no mass. All of radiography builds on these.

payload.sub: Mass one, mass one, almost no mass.
